notes: Seeded from define-tech-design evals cases #3, #4, #5, #6 — the four that exercise Stage-2 enumeration / gate / bundle-rejection / shared-decision behavior.

Added case #4 (waits-for-approval-before-filing-issues) to cover the new behavior that's specific to this split skill: filing one GitHub issue per approved ADR via gh issue create, but only after the human approves the list.

evals.name: gate-refuses-unreviewed-tech-requirements
evals.prompt: Tech-requirements.md exists for the self-hosted-application-platform capability at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md. Plan the ADRs.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to enumerate ADRs because reviewed_at is null. Should tell the user to review tech-requirements.md and set reviewed_at to today's ISO date. Should not produce any ADR list and should not file any issues.

assertions.name: refuses_to_enumerate
assertions.check: Response declines to enumerate ADRs / decisions because the gate is not satisfied.

assertions.name: names_the_gate_state
assertions.check: Response specifically calls out the reviewed_at value (null or stale) as the reason for stopping.

assertions.name: instructs_user_to_set_reviewed_at
assertions.check: Response tells the user to set reviewed_at to today's ISO date in tech-requirements.md.

assertions.name: no_issues_filed
assertions.check: No `gh issue create` invocation occurs (no issues created).

assertions.name: no_decision_list_proposed
assertions.check: Response does not propose a list of ADRs / decisions.

evals.name: enumerates-before-solving
evals.prompt: Tech-requirements.md is reviewed (reviewed_at: 2026-04-26, file unchanged since). Plan the ADRs for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. The file is at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT immediately start drafting an ADR or proposing options. Should first read tech-requirements.md, then enumerate the discrete decisions the requirements force as a list (titles + one-liners), each tied to specific TR-NNs. Should ask the user to confirm the list before filing issues.

assertions.name: produces_a_decision_list
assertions.check: Response contains a numbered or bulleted list of proposed ADRs (titles + one-liners), not a single ADR draft.

assertions.name: each_item_cites_TRs
assertions.check: Each proposed ADR item references the TR-NN identifiers it would address.

assertions.name: no_options_proposed
assertions.check: Response does not weigh options or propose a chosen option for any ADR.

assertions.name: asks_for_user_approval_before_filing
assertions.check: Response explicitly asks the user to confirm/edit the list before issues are filed.

assertions.name: no_premature_issue_filing
assertions.check: No `gh issue create` invocation occurs in this turn (since the user has not yet approved).

evals.name: rejects-bundled-adr
evals.prompt: For the self-hosted-application-platform capability, plan a single ADR called 'Tenant data architecture' that covers where tenant state lives, how it's partitioned, how it's accessed, and how it's backed up. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should push back on bundling. Should propose splitting into separate ADRs (storage location, partitioning, access pattern, backup strategy), explaining that bundling dilutes rationale and prevents superseding one decision without revisiting all of them. Should ask which to tackle first or propose an order.

assertions.name: refuses_the_bundle
assertions.check: Response declines to plan a single 'Tenant data architecture' ADR covering all four sub-decisions.

assertions.name: proposes_split_into_multiple_ADRs
assertions.check: Response proposes splitting into at least three separate ADRs (storage, partitioning, access, backup are the four expected).

assertions.name: explains_why_bundling_is_bad
assertions.check: Response explains that bundling dilutes rationale and/or prevents superseding sub-decisions independently.

assertions.name: no_issues_filed_yet
assertions.check: No issues are filed in this turn — the split proposal still needs user confirmation.

evals.name: flags-shared-cross-capability-decision
evals.prompt: For the self-hosted-application-platform capability, plan an ADR for how all platform services authenticate to each other — let's go with mTLS terminated at the Cloudflare edge with cert rotation every 30 days. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a cross-capability decision (touches Cloudflare topology and identity, affects every capability). Should refuse to plan it as a capability-scoped ADR and instead surface that it belongs in docs/content/r&d/adrs/ as a shared ADR (a separate flow). Should also note that 'go with mTLS' is the answer (a Stage-3 / define-adr concern, not enumeration).

assertions.name: recognizes_cross_capability_scope
assertions.check: Response identifies the decision as cross-capability / shared, not capability-scoped.

assertions.name: directs_to_shared_adr_namespace
assertions.check: Response says the shared ADR belongs in docs/content/r&d/adrs/ (and that this skill does not handle it).

assertions.name: does_not_file_a_capability_scoped_issue
assertions.check: No capability-scoped ADR issue is filed for this shared decision.

assertions.name: flags_premature_decision_or_offers_to_defer
assertions.check: Response either notes that 'go with mTLS' is choosing the answer (define-adr's job, not this skill's) OR offers to defer the shared ADR and proceed with the rest of the capability-scoped list.

evals.name: files-one-issue-per-adr-after-approval
evals.prompt: Tech-requirements.md is reviewed for self-hosted-application-platform (reviewed_at: 2026-04-26, file unchanged since). Plan the ADRs and file the issues — I trust your enumeration, just go.
evals.expected_output: Should still mirror back the enumerated list before filing — 'just go' does not bypass the human-confirmation gate. Once the user signals approval (this prompt's 'I trust your enumeration, just go' counts as approval), should file one GitHub issue per ADR via gh issue create, with bodies that reference the parent capability, the TR-NNs addressed, and that name define-adr as the next-step skill. Issues should be titled with the repo's `story(adr): ...` convention.

assertions.name: produces_decision_list
assertions.check: Response enumerates the proposed ADRs before filing.

assertions.name: files_one_issue_per_ADR
assertions.check: Skill invokes `gh issue create` once per enumerated ADR.

assertions.name: issue_titles_match_repo_convention
assertions.check: Filed issue titles follow the `story(adr): ...` form.

assertions.name: issue_bodies_cite_TR_NNs
assertions.check: Each filed issue body references the specific TR-NN identifiers the ADR will address.

assertions.name: issue_bodies_reference_define_adr
assertions.check: Each filed issue body names `define-adr` as the next-step skill that will author the ADR.

assertions.name: no_adr_files_written
assertions.check: No ADR markdown files are written under `adrs/` — issue filing is the only artifact at this stage.
